policy: Stress regime rules adjust macro-cycle posture by identifying what kind of pressure is driving the market. They prevent generic defensive allocation mistakes such as treating bonds, gold, and oil as interchangeable.

balanced.label: Balanced
balanced.meaning: No single stress type dominates. Asset posture should follow cycle phase and chart confirmation.

rate_pressure.label: Rate pressure
rate_pressure.meaning: Higher yields or weak duration pressure valuation multiples and long-duration growth.

tilt_adjustments.QQQ / growth: Reduce marginal adds; favor profitable quality
tilt_adjustments.Bonds / TLT: Watch, do not accumulate aggressively until rates roll over
tilt_adjustments.Gold: Small hedge if real-rate/geopolitical pressure confirms
tilt_adjustments.Oil / energy: Conditional if rate pressure is inflation-linked

volatility_stress.label: Volatility stress
volatility_stress.meaning: Volatility is rising enough to block casual risk deployment and force defensive posture.

liquidity_fragility.label: Liquidity fragility
liquidity_fragility.meaning: Speculative liquidity is weakening; high-beta and crypto-linked assets become vulnerable.

tilt_adjustments.New opportunities: Research only unless evidence is exceptional

sentiment_excess.label: Sentiment excess
sentiment_excess.meaning: Trend may remain strong, but forward return deteriorates as crowding and narrative confidence rise.

inflationary_supply_shock.label: Inflationary / supply shock
inflationary_supply_shock.meaning: Inflation or supply pressure changes the defensive mix; oil and real assets can outperform bonds.

tilt_adjustments.Bonds / TLT: Avoid aggressive duration until inflation cools

disinflationary_slowdown.label: Disinflationary slowdown
disinflationary_slowdown.meaning: Growth slows and inflation cools; bonds and quality duration can become attractive while cyclicals weaken.

credit_stress.label: Credit stress
credit_stress.meaning: Credit spreads or funding stress are deteriorating; capital preservation dominates.

tilt_adjustments.Bonds / TLT: Treasury duration may work if disinflationary
